codeReview.summary: The original refactor commit `6871d15` regressed the history badge by replacing the previous transcript-length fallback with `session.messageCount || 0`, so legacy sessions without `messageCount` rendered as `0 messages`. The fix commit `10f24fcc` restores the fallback in `history-list.ts` by using `session.messageCount ?? session.transcript?.length ?? 0`, which correctly covers stored sessions missing `messageCount` while preserving explicit zero values. I did not find any new code issues introduced by this targeted change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill still invites inaccurate `followedProcedure` reporting: this fix handoff marked `followedProcedure: true` even though the skill's required final validation steps were not all run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:96-107,130` requires `npm run test:unit`, `npm run test:integration`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build`, and says `followedProcedure: true` is only valid if all validation steps actually ran. Handoff `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T14-22-24-885Z__fix-history-messagecount-fallback__4351830d-2a78-4acc-a43c-e6f2311fcdd6.json` records only `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run check:repo-standards`, and `wc -l`, yet still sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` to true.

summary: Pass: this fix cleanly resolves the earlier non-blocking legacy message-count regression in the history list without introducing new code problems. I also noted the recurring shared-state gap where `refactor-worker` handoffs mark `followedProcedure: true` despite skipped required validation steps.
